channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Danish leader tells the US 'you cannot annex another country' as she visits Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Denmark's prime minister is wrapping up a three-day visit to Greenland on Friday after telling the U.S. “you cannot annex another country,” even with the argument that international security is at stake.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol from office Friday, ending his tumultuous presidency and setting up a new election, four months after he threw the nation into turmoil with an ill-fated declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In Tunisia, snails inch toward replacing red meat as people turn to cheaper protein
item.description: AKOUDA, Tunisia (AP) — In fields outside their hometown in central Tunisia, an increasing number of unemployed young men are seeking a new way to make a living, picking snails off of rocks and leaves and collecting them in large plastic bags to take to the local market to be sold.
item.dc:creator: MEHDI EL AREM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: MANKULAM, Sri Lanka (AP) — Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decadeslong separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: ERANGA JAYAWARDENA and KRISHAN FRANCIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Colleges say the Trump administration is using new tactics to expel international students
item.description: A crackdown on foreign students is alarming colleges, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Annie Ma And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: SENECA, Kansas (AP) — A Catholic priest was shot and killed Thursday by a man who approached him at his parish rectory in the town of Seneca, Kansas, church officials said. “I am heartbroken to share the tragic news of the death of Fr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Standing alongside the twisted steel tractors on his family farm in northeast Arkansas on Thursday, Danny Qualls looked on while friends and relatives helped him begin cleaning up.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man miraculously walks away after driving truck over cliff into Niagara Falls Gorge
item.description: A New York man crashed his truck through a fence, over a cliff and down into the Niagara Falls Gorge on Wednesday. Police said he was able to walk away from the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Stock market tumble impacts retirement savings; Florida economists explain
item.description: “This panic, or phase, is not going to last more than 30 days. The stock market, sooner than later, will bounce back,” said Tampa Economist Federico Alves.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: University of South Carolina student, 21, killed in hit-and-run crash involving illegal immigrant: DHS
item.description: Nathanial "Nate" G. Baker was killed after being struck on his motorcycle by a truck driven by Rosali Fernandez-Cruz, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: How to find out if your car is foreign-made
item.description: President Donald Trump recently announced a 25% tariff on all foreign-made vehicles and their parts—including those used in U.S. assembly plants.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, KRON4

item.title: Dog saved after falling over 100 feet at Colorado National Monument
item.description: What started as a dog owner's worst nightmare ended in a heartwarming reunion thanks in part to a multi-agency effort by Colorado responders.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KRON4

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Myanmar earthquake death toll rises to 3,145 as more bodies found
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from the earthquake that hit Myanmar nearly a week ago rose Thursday to 3,145 as search and rescue teams found more bodies, the military-led government said, and humanitarian aid groups scrambled to provide survivors medical care and shelter.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves prompt emergency in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to declare a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tornadoes, winds, flooding and hail: Arkansas wakes up to disastrous severe storm damage
item.description: Communities across Arkansas woke up to severe storm damage as the state continues to prepare for more potential severe weather later this week.
item.dc:creator: Cate Skinner, NewsNation

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk did a 'good job,' but it's time to go: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly tells Chris Cuomo that Elon Musk has accomplished what he sought to do but should now return to the private sector.
item.dc:creator: Matt Spears, NewsNation

item.title: 4 GOP senators vote with Democrats to undo Trump tariffs on Canada
item.description: A group of Republican senators voted along with Democrats on Wednesday for a resolution to undo President Trump's 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports, dealing an embarrassing blow to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father's clothes.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Man accused of breaking in, snatching chicken from ex-girlfriend's home
item.description: A man was arrested over the weekend after he was accused of breaking into his ex-girlfriend's home and stealing her pet chicken.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WGN 9

item.title: Monthly mortgage payments soar to record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans win Florida special elections in Trump strongholds by narrower margins than in 2024
item.description: ORMOND BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Republicans Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine won special elections Tuesday in two Florida congressional districts, bolstered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement to fill vacant seats in reliably Republican strongholds.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Democratic-backed candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court defeated a challenger endorsed by President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk on Tuesday, touting her victory as a win against powerful interests and cementing a liberal majority for at least three more years.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, WGN 9

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Glendale man among 4 soldiers killed on training mission in Lithuania
item.description: A soldier from Los Angeles County is among the four killed by a training exercise in Lithuania last month. The four soldiers were reported missing last week when their armored vehicle sank in a bog, the New York Times reports. "The four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Astronaut reunites with dogs after 9 months 'stuck' in space
item.description: Touching video shows NASA astronaut Suni Williams reuniting with her dogs after a longer-than-expected stay on board the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kidney donor detained by ICE before life-saving transplant can take place
item.description: Immigration rights activists are calling on ICE to release a Venezuelan migrant who was preparing to donate a kidney to his brother.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: It is the first time the Justice Department has sought to bring the death penalty since President Donald Trump returned to office in January.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in 17 states recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4
